titlestmt.titleproper: Joe Conzo, Jr. photographs and ephemera8091
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-05-19 00:11:28 -0400.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Joe Conzo, Jr. photographs and ephemera
did.abstract: Collection includes photographic film negatives, 4x6 and 8x10 prints, proof and contact sheets, large format finished prints, and associated ephemera. Many of the images have been digitized and arrived on hard drives.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to Box 4, Folder 3 restricted to permission of the curator. Negatives closed for research, access to those materials is available through the digital collection.

p.ref: Subseries I.a. Large Format Prints - Cold Crush Brothers
Subseries I.b. Large Format Prints - Bronx, N.Y. Scenes
Subseries I.c. 8 x 10 Prints - Modern, Reprints
Subseries I.d. 8 x 10 Prints - Contemporary to original photo date
Subseries I.e. 4 x 6 Prints - Cold Crush Brothers
Subseries I.f. 4 x 6 Prints - Bronx, N.Y. Scenes

emph.ref: Series II. Contact Sheets, Negatives, and Slides
Series IV. Born in the Bronx

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Joe Conzo, Jr. is an American photographer, born in the Bronx, New York City in 1963. In 1978, while attending South Bronx High School, Conzo became friends with members of the Cold Crush Brothers, an important and influential early Hip Hop group which included DJs Charlie Chase and Tony Tone and MCs Grandmaster Caz, JDL, Easy AD, and Almighty KayGee. Conzo became the group's personal photographer, documenting their live performances at the T-Connection, Disco Fever, Harlem World, the Ecstasy Garage, and the Hoe Avenue Boy's Club. He also took pictures of other Hip Hop artists and groups, including The Treacherous 3, The Fearless 4, and The Fantastic 5. As a result, he captured some of the earliest images of Hip Hop culture, along with every day street scenes and Latin music performances and events between 1977 and 1984. Joe Conzo is currently a Medical Technician with the New York City Fire Department, and is active in leadership of the EMTs labor union. He continues to photograph Hip Hop and other music performers both nationally and internationally.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Joe Conzo, Jr. photographs and ephemera, #8091. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Associated online resource: call number TR654.C66.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes photographic film negatives, 4x6 and 8x10 prints, proof and contact sheets, large format finished prints, and associated ephemera. Many of the images have been digitized and arrived on hard drives.

controlaccess.subject: Hip-hop dance.
Disc jockeys.
Rap musicians -- United States -- Biography.
Hip-hop -- United States -- History -- Pictorial works.
Hip-hop -- United States -- History.

did.unittitle: Large Format Prints - Cold Crush Brothers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posed Studio Shot, taken at United Bronx Parents

unittitle.emph: Tony Tone and Kool Herc at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tony Tone and Kool Herc posing with crossed arms backstage at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tony Tone looking through record crates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush Brothers on stage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush Brothers posing on the street

unittitle.emph: Fans of the Cold Crush Brothers vs. Fantastic Romantic 5 battle, Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three Fans with Cold Crush shirts at the famous 1981 battle between Cold Crush and the Fantastic Romantic Five.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers at Hoe Ave. Boys Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush strike a humorous pose on stage

unittitle.emph: DJ Charlie Chase Norman Thomas H.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charlie Chase with Tony Tone behind table with DJ equipment and record crates. In the background you see a rope separating the 'stage' from the crowd in the high school gym.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers at Wild Style shoot, Dixie Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush Bros. performing at Dixie Club while being filmed for Charlie Ahearn's film Wild Style.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Bros. and Fantastic Romantic 5 Wild Style rehearsal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush Bros. and Fantastic Romantic 5 rehearsing for the basketball court scene in Charlie Ahearn's film Wild Style. The basketball court was located at either Ryer Ave. and 182nd Street in the Bronx or at Intermediate School 115 (Morris Ave. and 184th).

unittitle.emph: Tony Tone, Afrika Bambaataa & Busy Bee at Bronx River, Zulu anniversary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tony Tone in foreground with visible turn tables and microphone, and Afrika Bambaataa and Busy Bee in background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collage of Cold Crush photos with text: "Cold Crush Fever is here! Catch it now."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The two Cold Crush DJs posing on street with crossed arms.

unittitle.emph: Almighty Kaygee & Disco King Mario (R.I.P) at Club Negril, N.Y.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kaygee stands in front of crowd, with Pioneering DJ Disco King Mario visible in crowd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J.D.L performing on stage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J.D.L smoking a joint in front of the Harlem World mural. Taken at the Treacherous Three Anniversary, 1982 or 1983.

unittitle.emph: Grand Master Caz, J.D.L. and Kaygee at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caz and Kaygee laughing as J.D.L goofs around at the Treacherous Three Anniversary, 1982 (or 1983?)

unittitle.emph: Grand Master Caz Norman Thomas H.S. Senior boat ride

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Cold Crush performance on the Norman Thomas High School senior trip to Bear Mountain on the Hudson River Day Line. (This line was later bought by the Circle Line).

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers and Treacherous Three

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Members of Cold Crush Bros. and Treacherous Three posing in front of crowd on Day Line Boat. Norman Thomas H.S. Senior boat ride

unittitle.emph: Tony Tone and [Easy] A.D. at Norman Thomas H.S

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tony Tone and Easy A.D. posing on stage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kay Gee throwing rolled up flyers/photos into the crowd. At the Harlem World Easter Show.

did.unittitle: Large Format Prints - Bronx, N.Y. Scenes

scopecontent.p: Boy standing in large puddle with skateboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Boy sitting on street with graffiti in background

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vacant lot behind abandoned buildings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Girl in crowd waving at camera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Treacherous Three posing in front of high school bleachers

unittitle.emph: Busy Bee at Bronx River, Zulu Nation Anniversary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Busy Bee on stage

unittitle.emph: Loose Bruce with Crazy Legs in background, Club Negril

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three B-Boys (Shack Crew? Harlem World Crew?) dancing in front of crowd. Crazy Legs is visible in the background watching the performance.

unittitle.emph: Loose Bruce with Crazy Legs in background, Club Negril

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Framed duplicate

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers, South Bronx High School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush performing at the 1980 South Bronx High School prom. This was Joe Conzo's prom.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush posing in suits in front of Harlem World mural

did.physdesc: Inscribed "To Johan - Thanks for everything! Joe Conzo 2004"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush lined up posing in suits in front of Harlem World mural

unittitle.emph: J.D.L. and Grandmaster Caz, Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J.D.L. and Caz (in Rick James-style leather outfit) sharing a microphone onstage

unittitle.emph: Kool Moe Dee and Grandmaster Caz, Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kool Moe Dee and Caz in the crowd at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J.D.L smoking a joint in front of the Harlem World mural. Taken at the Treacherous Three Anniversary, 1982 or 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Easy A.D., Kaygee, and [J.D.L.?] raising their arms in the air with their backs to the crowd

unittitle.emph: Printouts of photos from disc entitled "Tony Tone polaroids"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Color photos of Tony Tone and others, possibly taken by Angelo King

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers and South Bronx

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various prints depicting Cold Crush Brothers performances and shots of the South Bronx

did.unittitle: 8 x 10 Prints - Contemporary to original photo date

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Broken fire hydrant and empty bottle of malt liquor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posed shot of Joe holding flashbulbs with a hat covering his face

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Newman chasing Joe and other protestors off of the set of the film Fort Apache, The Bronx. This was Joe's first published photograph; it appeared in the New York Post, March 25 1980. The film set was located at Eagle Ave. and Westchester Ave. in the Bronx. Community activists were protesting the film's negative depiction of minorities and of the Bronx in general. This print is accompanied by a mounted photocopy of the New York Post article with note from "Reyes II" of the UBP (United Bronx Parents) Photography Workshop congratulating Joe on its publication.

unittitle.emph: Vigil for John Lennon at Strawberry Fields, Central Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mourners for John Lennon holding hands

unittitle.emph: Ben Gazzara at a protest of Fort Apache: The Bronx, Waldorf-Astoria Hotel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The actor Ben Gazzara and an unidentified woman photographed outside the Waldorf-Astoria Hotel during a protest of the film Fort Apache, The Bronx.

unittitle.emph: Subway platform, Prospect Ave. and Westchester Ave.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Man reading a newspaper while waiting for the train at the Prospect Ave. subway stop in the Bronx

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Rachel; the back of her sister Abigail's head is also visible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posed shot of Sonia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Matte board from which a mounted photograph has been removed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of three children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of three children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of unidentified woman

unittitle.emph: Tony Tone, LA Sunshine, and Grandmaster Caz, Harlem World

did.physdesc: Signed on the front by Tony Tone, LA Sunshine, and Grandmaster Caz; dated on the back "July 3, 1981: Battle"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tony Tone and Grandmaster Caz of the Cold Crush Brothers pose with LA Sunshine of the Treacherous Three

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers, South Bronx High School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush Brothers and the audience at the South Bronx High School gym

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clerk behind the counter inside Botanica -- a store that sold religious supplies (rosaries, candles, etc.)

did.physdesc: Caption in red marker: These are the eighties and it [sic] all about C.C.: Cold Crush!!

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posed picture of Cold Crush in suits

unittitle.emph: Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posed studio shot of Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five; photographer unknown. Sugarhill Records publicity photo.

did.physdesc: Note in red marker on back reads "Marie Moore, Amsterdam New, 2340 8th Ave., 5th Flr - By [Hard?]". Blue pen note reads "Grandmaster Flash"; possibly Flash's signature.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kurtis Blow and Melle Mel looking at a magazine article on Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five, possibly at the Amsterdam New, 2340 8th Ave, 4th floor. Photograph by Gary Gershoff.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers and fans at T-Connection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early Joe Conzo photos of Cold Crush-- possibly the first. Also several shots of people from the audience (friends of Joe Conzo); one shot of Tony Tone with his mother; one shot of Cold Crush's DJ equipment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Fearless Four onstage; Money Ray, Joe Conzo, Easy A.D. and friends in the audience.

unittitle.emph: Cold crush Brothers, South Bronx H.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush performance at South Bronx High School in 1980 with additional pictures of the Treacherous 3, Kool Moe Dee, Cracker Jack of the Casanova Crew, and King. A photograph from this show was used (without permission) in the film CB4.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers, South Bronx H.S. Prom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush performing at the 1980 South Bronx High School prom. Joe Conzo, Easy A.D., and Tony Tone attended South Bronx HS; this was Joe's prom.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush van ride (Tony Tone's van) upstate NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Joe Conzo, Cold Crush Brothers, Nana (Tony Tone's cousin), King (Tony Tone's brother), Money Ray, and [Kool Moe Dee], driving upstate in Tony Tone's van. Joe claims that Kool Moe Dee was not present in the van, so we are waiting for identification on the last individual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush performance at the Skatin' Palace in 1981.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush backstage at the Skatin' Palace, 1981.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush and Loose Bruce, Club Negril's

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shots of Cold Crush performing and some shots of b-boy Loose Bruce dancing. Before Cold Crush J.D.L. and Grand Master Caz performed together as the Notorious Two. There are several silhouette shots of Caz and J.D.L. together in this group, and though this is a Cold Crush performance, Joe Conzo refers to these photos in this group as shots of the Notorious Two.

unittitle.emph: Charlie Chase, Afrika Bambaataa, Grand Mixer DST, Kips Bay Boys Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charlie Chase, Afrika Bambaataa, and Grand Mixer DST at the Kips Bay Boys Club, 1981.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush, Electricians' Hall (Amityville, Long Island)
Cold Crush at the Fever (167th and Jerome)
Cold Crush Harlem World Easter Show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This group of photos is a very detailed recording of the 1981 performance of Cold Crush at Harlem World. Shots from this show are identifiable because of the use of a smoke machine onstage.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush and the Treacherous Three, Norman Thomas H.S

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush and the Treacherous Three perform at Norman Thomas High School in 1981. Also several posed shots of both groups in front of the gym bleachers.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush upstairs at Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush rehearsing upstairs at Harlem World before a show in 1981.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush, Hoe Ave. Boys Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush performance at Roosevelt H.S. with pictures of crowd and members.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers and Fantastic Romantic Five 'Wild Style' Rehearsals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cold Crush Brothers and Fantastic Romantic 5 rehearsing for Charlie Ahearn's film 'Wild Style' basketball court scene. The basketball court was located at either Ryer Ave. and 182nd Street in the Bronx OR at Intermediate School 115 (Morris Ave. and 184th). Some shots focus on Kevie Kev and Whipper Whip (of the Fantastic Five); Charlie Ahearn and Fab Five Freddy are also visible.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers and Charlie Ahearn signing the 'Wild Style' contract, Dixie Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cold Crush and Charlie Ahearn sign the contract for Ahearn's film Wild Style.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers Hoe Ave Boys Club
Cold Crush Brothers and Fantastic Romantic Five battle, Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pictures of the Cold Crush Brothers performance in the famous battle with the Fantastic Romantic Five.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush Brothers, South Bronx High School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tony Tone, DJ Red Alert, J.D.L., Grand Master Caz, Mr. Biggs (of the Soul Sonic Force) and Busy Bee at the 1981 Zulu Nation Anniversary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three portrait photos taken on Prospect Ave. and 156th street in the Bronx.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large number of posed group, solo, and duo shots of the Cold Crush Brothers. These portraits were taken in a makeshift studio in the back room of United Bronx Parents in the Bronx.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posed and performance shots of Cold Crush Brothers (in suits)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wide shots of the stage during a Cold Crush performance at Harlem World

unittitle.emph: Norman Thomas High School Senior Boat Ride

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Cold Crush performance on the Norman Thomas High School senior trip to Bear Mountain on the Hudson River Day Line. (This line was later bought by the Circle Line). Also included: one shot of LA Sunshine (of the Treacherous Three) with members of Cold Crush; several shots of students/audience on boat

unittitle.emph: Treacherous Three Anniversary - Cold Crush Brothers, Jazzy Ace and D-Ski, Wayne and Charlie; Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shots of performances by Cold Crush, Jazzy Ace and D-Ski, and hip hop ventriloquist act Wayne and Charlie at the Treacherous Three anniversary in either 1982 or 1983. Also: two pictures of Tony Tone's brother King in the crowd.

unittitle.emph: Cold Crush - Wild Style Filming, Dixie Club,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These pictures show the filming of a scene for Wild Style. The film crew is visible in the crowd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos of unidentified individuals from a UBP picnic in 1978. Also one picture of Joe's girlfriend Teresa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Newman chasing Joe and other protestors off of the set of the film Fort Apache, The Bronx. This was Joe's first published photograph; it appeared in the New York Post, March 25 1980. The film set was located at Eagle Ave. and Westchester Ave. in the Bronx. Community activists were protesting the film's negative depiction of minorities and of the Bronx in general.

unittitle.emph: Tito Puente and Machito outdoor performance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos of Tito Puente and Machito on stage outdoors in the Bronx. Several photos of Tito and Machito performing, some shots of the crowd, and two photos of filmmaker Carlos Ortiz and his film crew. Ortiz was one of Joe Conzo's mentors, and directed a documentary about Machito.

unittitle.emph: Vigil for John Lennon at Strawberry Fields, Central Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two young girls mourning John Lennon in Central Park

unittitle.emph: Mean Machine' outdoor event, 3rd Ave Hub

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos of Mean Machine during an outdoor event sponsored by the radio station 92 KTV at the 3rd Ave Hub in the Bronx. The photos include a few pictures of the group onstage, but most are of individuals in the audience.

unittitle.emph: Government Cheese handout, United Bronx Parents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Residents of the Bronx waiting in line to pick up free government cheese at United Bronx Parents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos of various locations in the Bronx in various locations, particularly along Prospect Avenue above 149th St. Some of the photos depict early graffiti and street art. Many street portraits of unidentified individuals. One print depicts a 1978 protest of funding cuts for sports programs in South Bronx high schools. One shot of a vacant lot at Westchester Ave. and Brook Ave. was used as the cover image for the book Next Stop: Growing Up Wild Style by Ivan Sanchez (Touchstone, 2008).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos of everyday street scenes scattered all over the Bronx. Photos depicting stores, street vendors, people walking, and children playing. There are also several shots of a film crew working on an unidentified documentary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These photos depict demolished buildings and vacant lots-- the devastation of the South Bronx. Also included are photos from the Cloisters in Manhattan, Lincoln Hospital, and the 1981 or 1982 Puerto Rican Day parade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This roll includes Joe's earliest black and white shots-- pictures of his classmates at Junior H.S 149 from 1976. Also included are pictures of Joe's girlfriend Teresa and her nieces, Joe's girlfriend Maria, Abigail and Rachel (two sisters from the Bronx), Sonya, Tiny and Herman (Tiny is the daughter of Carmen Arroyo, the first Hispanic woman in NY assembly), Sandy Santiago and Dr. Sporano from Lincoln Hospital, Carlos, Cappy, Roseann Pascale (a college friend of Joe's who now produces the television show Miami Ink), David, and several unidentified children from the South Bronx. The last photo in this group is two unidentified individuals who may have been radio DJs; they seem to be in a radio station or record store.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These portraits are of various people in or outside the office of United Bronx Parents including. Named individuals include Reuben and Yvette, Evelina Antonetty (Founder of U.B.P. and Joe Conzo's grandmother), and Donald Antonetty. There are also several color shots from a UBP party in 1976, including a shot of two vendors who provided food for a summer lunch program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These photos depict places and people from Joe Conzo's life in the late 1970s and early 1980s. There are several self-portraits, photos of his son Angel's baptism, his ex-wife Gina Martinez, his first car (a 1976 Chevy Malibu), his cat, his apartment, Lorraine Montenegro (Joe's mother) and her boyfriend Sailor, a basement clubhouse that he shared with his friends, Joe's girlfriend Doris, Joe's family members Donnie (uncle), Eric Kane (brother), Lorraine Montenegro (mother), and Anita (aunt), Joe and friends at Coney Island, Joe's "wedding" to an unknown woman in 1981, Joe and Rolando Arroyo (son of Carmen Arroyo) at Michelangelo Apartments in 1980, and one photo from the South Bronx High School 1980 senior trip to a dude ranch in upstate New York. There are also several self-portraits (including one taken at the studio of Tom Rose Joe's mentor, at Riverside and 92nd St. in Manhattan). Also included are some of Joe's lighting experiments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The contact sheets are numbered 1-35, but the photos are in no particular order on them. Joe's negatives were kept in a box in his grandmother's house for several years, and they were not organized before the 4x6 prints were developed.

unittitle.emph: Hip Hop Doc - Kool Moe Dee

did.unittitle: Series II. Contact Sheets, Negatives, and Slides

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: File subjects are listed out for contact sheets and negatives as labeled by Joe Conzo. Numerous unlabeled sheets are included in each case and are not listed out in the guide.
Most contact sheets have been scanned and are available through ARTstor's Shared Shelf Commons. Item ID number ranges for each case are listed out where applicable.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Negatives closed for research, access to those materials is available through the digital collection.

did.unittitle: Norman Thomas H.S. boat ride - Harlem World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JC_CS_071 - JC_CS_107

General shots - school - Purchase College - school walkout

Atlantic City H.R. c/o Roberto G.
Laura's birthday party at Chico East

scopecontent.p: JC_CS_001 - JC_CS_047

did.unittitle: Norman Thomas Boat Ride - Cold Crush Brothers
BX River, Busy Bee, CC4, Zulu

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JC_CS_197 - JC_CS_222

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JC_CS_147 - JC_CS_172

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JC_CS_173 - JC_CS_196

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JC_CS_048 - JC_CS_070

did.unittitle: Tito Puente Celebrity Smoke Out TP Rest
Tito Puente Latin Jazz Beacon Theater

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: JC_CS_223 - JC_CS_253

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Folder 3 from box 4 is restricted to the permission of the curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of handwritten lyrics and an unsigned copy of a 1982 contract between CC4 and Frost Belt International Recording Enterprises.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes article on early hip hop featuring Conzo photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers for performances at T-Connection, Ecstasy Garage Disco, and Skatin' Palace.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains magazines related to latinx culture, as well as an issue of "That 70s Show: New York City in the 1970s" which includes writing and photos by Conzo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipping from March 25, 1980 issue of the New York Post, featuring Joe Conzo's photo of Paul Newman. Includes inscription: "UBP's first published student photographer, Joey Kane. Congratulations from the UBP Photography Workshop! - Reyes II"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to Box 4, Folder 3 restricted to permission of the curator.

did.unittitle: Series IV. Born in the Bronx
Prints from "The Breaks: Stylin' and Profilin'"
